mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the threshold, or signal Sharpe ratio is negative over walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline by >2% over 60 days in >70% of triggered periods, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output, which translates to positive equity returns over the next month during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >50% of months when industrial production rises during expansion regimes, or the signal loses predictive power when GDP growth exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX yields positive 5-day returns in >65% of months following inflation spikes above the threshold, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirm a strengthening labor market, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative quarterly returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.5% signals robust aggregate demand and corporate profit expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month during stable growth regimes.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >50% of quarters following strong GDP prints, or the signal fails to maintain a positive Rank IC when inflation exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the industrial production index indicate contracting manufacturing activity and weakening economic momentum, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in >60% of weeks following industrial production declines, or the signal predictive accuracy falls below random chance over a 2-year rolling window.
